summary: In this very early blog post from 2001, a young DHH reflects on being one month into his business/computer science education and already questioning whether the destination is right for him. He finds courses like managerial economics and macro economics intellectually challenging but doubts he could ever see himself in a role that requires those skills. It's a brief, introspective moment capturing the seeds of his eventual path away from traditional business careers toward software and entrepreneurship.
key_points: DHH is early in a combined business/computer science degree and already questioning its relevance to his future
He distinguishes between finding courses intellectually stimulating versus wanting to use those skills professionally
The post foreshadows his eventual rejection of conventional corporate career paths
money_quotes: One month down, leaving five years minus one month to go, and I'm questioning the destination of that business/computer science education of mine.
Could I ever image myself in a position that required the use of such skills? I'm doubtful.
key_insight: Even at the very start of his formal education, DHH sensed that conventional business roles weren't for him — a doubt that would eventually define his unconventional career.
